'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948年11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971年}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973年}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991年}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948年11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971年}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973年}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991年}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948年11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971年}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973年}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992年}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991年}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html Kokugakuin University] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ March 9, 2019: Final Lecture by Professor Shōji Okada, Faculty of Shinto Studies] (Accessed February 2022)

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948年11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year }} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973年}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year }}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948年11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year }} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year }}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year }}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year }} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year }}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year }}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year }}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司[[ja:岡田莊司]]) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year }} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year }}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year }}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year }}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year }} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year }}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=draft}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=draft}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=draft}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=draft}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=draft}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=draft}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|12=draft}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|12=draft}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year|12=draft}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=draft}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=draft}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=draft}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=draft}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=draft}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=draft}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=draft}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=draft}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=draft}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=draft}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|12=draft}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=draft}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=draft}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=draft}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=draft}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=draft}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=draft}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|12=draft}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|12=draft}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year|12=draft}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=draft}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=draft}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=draft}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=draft}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=draft}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=draft}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=draft}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=draft}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=draft}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=draft}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|12=draft}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|12=draft}}

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=draft}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=draft}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=draft}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=draft}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=draft}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=draft}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|12=draft}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|12=draft}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|12=draft}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year|12=draft}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=draft}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=draft}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=draft}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=draft}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=draft}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|12=draft}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=draft}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=draft}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=draft}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=draft}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=draft}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=draft}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|12=draft}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

revision.text:
{{存命人物の出典明記|date=2016年7月8日 (金) 09:06 (UTC)}} '''岡田 莊司'''（おかだ しょうじ、[[1948年]][[11月22日]] - ）は、[[日本]]の[[神道学者]]。[[学位]]は、[[博士（歴史学）]]（[[1995年]]）。[[國學院大學]][[神道文化学部]]名誉教授<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ 2019年06月05日(水) 名誉教授称号授与式]2022年2月閲覧</ref>。
==略歴== [[鎌倉市]][[雪ノ下]]生まれ。1971年國學院大學[[國學院大学#組織|文学部]][[史学科]]卒、1973年同[[國學院大學#研究科|大学院文学研究科]][[修士課程]]修了。1981年國學院大學[[専任講師]]、1984年[[助教授]]、1992年同大学神道文化学部教授。2019年定年退職、[[名誉教授]]の称号を授与される<ref name=名誉教授/>。
1984年「吉田[[卜部氏]]の成立」で神道宗教学会賞受賞。1991年[[大倉精神文化研究所]]研究員<ref>『現代日本人名録』2002年</ref>。1995年「[[平安時代]]の[[国家]]と[[祭祀]]」で[[博士]]（[[歴史学]]）の学位を取得。
==著書== *『大嘗の祭り』[[学生社]] 1990 *『平安時代の国家と祭祀』[[続群書類従完成会]] 1996 *『大嘗祭と古代の祭祀』[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b437087.html][[吉川弘文館]] 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 *『古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系』[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b596726.html]吉川弘文館 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 *『中世神道と神社の信仰体系』[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b10089695.html]吉川弘文館 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
===共編著=== *『現代人のための[[祝詞]] [[大祓詞]]の読み方』1訂版 [[阪本是丸]]共監修 [[大島敏史]],[[中村幸弘]]編著 [[右文書院]] 2000 *『古代諸国[[神社]][[神階]]制の研究』編 [[岩田書院]] 2002 *『古代[[出雲大社]]の祭儀と[[神殿]]』[[椙山林継]],[[牟禮仁]],[[錦田剛志]],[[松尾充晶]]共著 学生社 2005 *『神道と日本文化』[[安蘇谷正彦]],椙山林繼,[[中西正幸]],[[茂木栄]],[[茂木貞純]]共著 [[兵庫県神社庁]]編 [[戎光祥出版]] 2006 *『日本神道史』小林宣彦共編 吉川弘文館 2010、 増補新版[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b557738.html] 2021年11月 ISBN 9784642083959 * {{Cite book |和書|year=2011-05|title=プレステップ神道学|publisher=[[弘文堂]]|isbn=9784335000799}} *『事典神社の歴史と祭り』[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b107930.html][[笹生衛]]共編 吉川弘文館 2013 ISBN 9784642080859 *『全国「[[一の宮]]」めぐり 69の神社で、ふるさとの神さまに出会う旅』監修 [[平凡社]]「別冊太陽 太陽の地図帖」 2104 *『神仏関係考 古代・中世・近世・近現代』[[加瀬直弥]],[[嵯峨井建]],[[藤田大誠]],[[阪本是丸]]共著 [[神社新報]]社「鎮守の杜ブックレット」 2016 *『事典 古代の祭祀と年中行事』[https://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b383503.html]編 吉川弘文館 2019 ISBN 9784642014786
===校注=== *『[[神道大系]] 神社編 20 鶴岡』豊原武共校注、神道大系編纂会 1979 *『神道大系 古典註釈編 8 [[中臣祓]]註釈』校注、神道大系編纂会 1985 *『神道大系 論説編 9 [[吉田神道|卜部神道]] 下』校注、神道大系編纂会 1991 *『神道古典 [[大須観音|真福寺善本]]叢刊 神道篇1』[[臨川書店]] 2019。編者代表
==論文== *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 Cinii]
== 関連項目 == * [[國學院大學の人物一覧]]
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html 國學院大學] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ 2019年03月09日(土) 岡田莊司神道文化学部教授最終講義] 2022年2月閲覧
{{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:おかた しようし}} [[Category:神道学者]] [[Category:日本古代史学者]] [[Category:國學院大學の教員]] [[Category:大倉精神文化研究所の人物]] [[Category:博士（歴史学）取得者]] [[Category:國學院大學出身の人物]] [[Category:神奈川県出身の人物]] [[Category:1948年生]] [[Category:存命人物]]

revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura City|qq=}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1971|qq=}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1981|qq=}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1991|qq=}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html Kokugakuin University] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ March 9, 2019: Final Lecture by Professor Shōji Okada, Faculty of Shinto Studies] (Accessed February 2022)

<!--Okada Chuangji--><!--Okada Chuangji-->

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura City|qq=}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1971|qq=}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1981|qq=}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1991|qq=}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ({{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura City|qq=}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|ja|1971年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1971|qq=}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in {{ill|1973|ja|1973年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1973|qq=}}. In {{ill|1981|ja|1981年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1981|qq=}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|ja|1992年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1992|qq=}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|ja|2019年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|ja|1984年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1984|qq=}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|ja|1991年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1991|qq=}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933

{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:博士（歴史学）取得者
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:大倉精神文化研究所の人物
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki
