[[ja:岡田莊司]] [[sn:Okada Chuangji]] [[en:Okada Chuangji]]
<!--Okada Chuangji--><!--Okada Chuangji--> [[Category:博士（歴史学）取得者]] [[Category:國學院大學の教員]] [[Category:Kokugakuin University alumni]] [[Category:大倉精神文化研究所の人物]] [[Category:日本古代史学者]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:神奈川県出身の人物
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html Kokugakuin University] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ March 9, 2019: Final Lecture by Professor Shōji Okada, Faculty of Shinto Studies] (Accessed February 2022)

[[ja:岡田莊司]] [[sn:Okada Chuangji]]
<!--Okada Chuangji--><!--Okada Chuangji--> [[Category:博士（歴史学）取得者]] [[Category:Faculty of Kokugakuin University]] [[Category:Kokugakuin University alumni]] [[Category:大倉精神文化研究所の人物]] [[Category:日本古代史学者]] [[Category:Births in Kanagawa prefecture]] [[Category:神道学者]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html Kokugakuin University] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ March 9, 2019: Final Lecture by Professor Shōji Okada, Faculty of Shinto Studies] (Accessed February 2022)

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:日本古代史学者]] into [[Category:Scholars of Ancient Japanese History]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue === Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大倉精神文化研究所の人物]] into [[Category:People of the Okura Spiritual and Cultural Research Institute]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|qq=}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|qq=}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|qq=}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|qq=}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|qq=}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|qq=}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991
== Articles == *[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/all?q=%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%B0%E8%8E%8A%E5%8F%B8&range=0&nrid=&count=100&sortorder=2&type=1 CiNii database] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|ja|國學院大學の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokugakuin University Affiliates|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kokugakuin.ac.jp/graduate/in03_00005.html Kokugakuin University] * [http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event_extramural/2019/03/09/173755/ March 9, 2019: Final Lecture by Professor Shōji Okada, Faculty of Shinto Studies] (Accessed February 2022)

{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933

{{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|qq=}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|qid=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|qid=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|qq=|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|qid=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|qid=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|qq=|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|qq=|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|1=Masayuki Nakanishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367061}}, {{ill|Sakae based on wikidata|WD=Q11495343}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|1=Mamoru Sasō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699778}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
