<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|1=Koremaru Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656823}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Ōshima|ja|大島敏史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshishi Ōshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yukihiro Nakamura|ja|中村幸弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukihiro Nakamura|1=Yukihiro Nakamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365560}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|1=Uibun Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55534121}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|1=Iwata Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596820}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|1=Shigetsugu Sugiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20040793}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|1=Masahiko Asoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451445}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|1=Masayuki Nakanishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367061}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|1=Sadasumi Motegi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21702496}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|1=Jukōshō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495343}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|1=Mamoru Sasō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699778}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|1=Koremaru Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656823}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ([[:en:1995|1995]]) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura City]]. He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

He graduated in [[:en:1971|1971]] from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. In [[:en:1981|1981]], he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in [[:en:1984|1984]], and a full professor in [[:en:1992|1992]]. He retired in [[:en:2019|2019]], receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in [[:en:1984|1984]] for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In [[:en:1991|1991]], he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in [[:en:1995|1995]] for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.

In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20039179]] Wikidata name: Shōji Okada [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ({{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=1973}}. In {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|1=Koremaru Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656823}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Nakamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365560}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|1=Uibun Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55534121}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|1=Iwata Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596820}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|1=Shigetsugu Sugiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20040793}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|1=Masahiko Asoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451445}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|1=Masayuki Nakanishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367061}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|1=Sadasumi Motegi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21702496}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|1=Jukōshō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495343}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|1=Mamoru Sasō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699778}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859
=== Annotations === * *Shinto Taikei, Shrine Edition 20: Tsurugaoka* (神道大系 神社編 20 鶴岡), co-annotated with {{ill|Takeshi Toyohara|ja|豊原武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Toyohara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1979 * *Shinto Taikei, Classical Annotation Edition 8: Nakatomi Harai* (神道大系 古典註釈編 8 中臣祓註釈), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1985 * *Shinto Taikei, Essay Edition 9: Urabe Shinto (Lower)* (神道大系 論説編 9 吉田神道 下), annotated, Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee, 1991

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915}}}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ({{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=1973}}. In {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|1=Koremaru Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656823}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Nakamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365560}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|1=Uibun Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55534121}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|1=Iwata Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596820}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|1=Shigetsugu Sugiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20040793}}, {{ill|Hitoshi invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|1=Masahiko Asoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451445}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|1=Masayuki Nakanishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367061}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|1=Sadasumi Motegi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21702496}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|1=Jukōshō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495343}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|1=Mamoru Sasō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699778}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|1=Kamakura}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915|1=Researcher}}}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=Doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ({{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Q125120417|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City|1=Kamakura}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}. In {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru Sakamoto|ja|阪本是丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zemaru Sakamoto|1=Koremaru Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656823}}, co-edited by {{ill|Toshishi Nakamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365560}}, {{ill|Uibun Shoin|ja|右文書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uibun Shoin|1=Q55534121|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55534121}}, 2000 * *Studies on the Shrine and Kami Rank System in Ancient Provinces* (古代諸国神社神階制の研究), edited by {{ill|Iwata Shoin|ja|岩田書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata Shoin|1=Iwata Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596820}}, 2002 * *Rituals and Halls of the Ancient Izumo Taisha Shrine* (古代出雲大社の祭儀と神殿), co-authored with {{ill|Hayatsugu Sugiyama|ja|椙山林継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatsugu Sugiyama|1=Shigetsugu Sugiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20040793}}, {{ill|Hitoshi Mure|ja|牟禮仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Mure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja|錦田剛志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyoshi Nishikida|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja|松尾充晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuaki Matsuo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gakuseisha, 2005 * *Shinto and Japanese Culture* (神道と日本文化), co-authored by {{ill|Masahiko Asoyani|ja|安蘇谷正彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masahiko Asoyani|1=Masahiko Asoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451445}}, Hayatsugu Sugiyama, {{ill|Masayuki Nakanishi|ja|中西正幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayuki Nakanishi|1=Masayuki Nakanishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367061}}, {{ill|Sakae Mogi|ja|茂木栄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Mogi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sadazumi Mogi|ja|茂木貞純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadazumi Mogi|1=Sadasumi Motegi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21702496}}, Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association, {{ill|Eibunkō Shō Publishing|ja|戎光祥出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eibunkō Shō Publishing|1=Jukōshō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495343}}, 2006 * *A Dictionary of Shrines' Histories and Festivals* (事典神社の歴史と祭り), co-edited with {{ill|Mamorue Sasaō|ja|笹生衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamorue Sasaō|1=Mamoru Sasō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21699778}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2013 ISBN 9784642080859

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:岡田莊司]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|1=Kamakura}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915|1=Researcher}}}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=Doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ({{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Q125120417|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City|1=Kamakura}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}. In {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=1948; {{birth date|1948|11|22}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|1=Kamakura}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|researcher|WD=Q1650915|1=Researcher}}}} '''Okada Chuangji''' (岡田莊司) was a Japanese historian of Shinto
{{BLP sources|date=July 8, 2016}} '''Shōji Okada''' (岡田 莊司, born {{ill|November 22, 1948|ja|1948th year 11月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 22, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto scholar|ja|神道学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神道学|1=Shinto studies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q114567052}}. He holds a {{ill|Doctorate in History|ja|博士（歴史学）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doctorate in History|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博士|1=Doctorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849697}} ({{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}) and is an emeritus professor at the {{ill|Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學神道文化学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faculty of Shinto Studies, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 國學院大學神道文化学部・大学院文学研究科神道学・宗教学専攻|1=Q125120417|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125120417}}<ref name=名誉教授>[http://pr.kokugakuin.ac.jp/event/2019/06/05/130737/ June 5, 2019, Conferment Ceremony of the Title of Professor Emeritus] Accessed February 2022.</ref>.
== Biography == Born in {{ill|Yukinoshita|ja|雪ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukinoshita|1=Yukinoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655085}}, {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City|1=Kamakura}}. He graduated in {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} from the {{ill|Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学文学部史学科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Department of History, Faculty of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and completed his master’s degree at the {{ill|Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大学大学院文学研究科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Graduate School of Letters, Kokugakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1973|en|1973|WD=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}. In {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}, he became a lecturer at Kokugakuin University, an associate professor in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}}, and a full professor in {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}. He retired in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, receiving the title of professor emeritus<ref name=名誉教授/>.
He won the Shinto Religious Studies Prize in {{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}} for his work on the formation of the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|卜部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}. In {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}}, he became a researcher at the {{ill|Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|ja|大倉精神文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura Institute for Spiritual Culture|1=Okura Institute for the Study of Spiritual Culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432442}}<ref>'Contemporary Japanese Biographical Directory' (2002)</ref>. He earned his doctorate in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} for his thesis on {{ill|The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja|平安時代の国家と祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The State and Rituals of the Heian Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Publications == * *The Daijōsai Ceremony* (大嘗の祭り), {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1990 * *The State and Rituals of the Heian Period* (平安時代の国家と祭祀), {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1996 * *The Daijōsai and Ancient Rituals* (大嘗祭と古代の祭祀), {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 2019 ISBN 9784642083508 * *Ancient Japanese Emperors and the Ritual System* (古代天皇と神祇の祭祀体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2022 ISBN 9784642046688 * *Medieval Shinto and the Faith System of Shrines* (中世神道と神社の信仰体系), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2024 ISBN 9784642029933
=== Co-Authored and Edited Works === * *How to Read Norioto and Ōharae Kotoba for Modern People* (現代人のための祝詞 大祓詞の読み方), 1st rev. ed., co-supervised by {{ill|Zemaru
